title: Install Timing Pulley On Gearbox Shaft After Replacement
summary: Install the timing pulley assembly onto the gearbox shaft by loosely assembling the taper lock bushing into the pulley, selecting the correct set screw holes, aligning the keyway with the gearbox shaft key if present, positioning the pulley 8 mm (0.315 in.) from the gearbox pilot face, tightening the set screws alternately to 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb), reinstalling panels as needed, and restarting the operator station using the referenced procedure.
when_to_use: Use after timing pulley replacement when reinstalling the timing pulley assembly onto the gearbox shaft and the source procedure on page 184 is the governing installation reference.
not_for: Do not use as a complete safety isolation procedure; the source excerpt does not provide full isolation or guarded-access prerequisites.
Do not use when the pulley cannot be aligned with the gearbox shaft key or the documented 8 mm spacing cannot be achieved.
Do not use when the set screws cannot be tightened in the documented alternating pattern to 5.6 Nm.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the mechanical assembly, shaft fitment, spacing, torque application, panel reinstallation, and operator station restart reference steps described by the source.

safety_notes: This source excerpt is installation-only and does not provide full safety isolation or guarded-access prerequisites.
Use higher-skill support review before execution because prerequisite safety controls are not included in the excerpt.
Use the documented set screw torque of 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb).
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the timing pulley and gearbox shaft
Torque tool capable of 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb)
Access to top and/or bottom side panels
Referenced procedure: Starting The Operator Station

access_required: Access to the timing pulley and gearbox shaft
Access to top and/or bottom side panels
system_preconditions: Timing pulley replacement has progressed to the installation stage.
Gearbox shaft is available for pulley installation.

visual_references.description: Timing pulley figure showing set screws, 1108 taper-lock bushing, timing pulley, gearbox pilot face, and 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap.

steps.title: Loosely assemble taper lock bushing into pulley
steps.purpose: Prepare the pulley and bushing assembly for correct hole alignment before installation on the shaft.
steps.instruction: Loosely assemble the taper lock bushing into the pulley and align all three holes.
steps.expected_result: The taper lock bushing is seated in the pulley loosely enough to allow alignment, and all three holes are aligned.
steps.healthy_condition: All three holes are aligned and the assembly remains adjustable for the next step.
steps.failure_condition: The bushing and pulley holes do not align or the assembly cannot be loosely fitted.

actions.label: Assemble bushing into pulley
actions.exact_action: Loosely assemble the taper lock bushing into the pulley and align all three holes.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing pulley and 1108 taper-lock bushing relationship and labeled assembly components.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the bushing cannot be assembled into the pulley with all three holes aligned.

source_evidence.text: Loosely assemble the taper lock bushing into the pulley and align all three holes.
Figure 7-20 includes labeled references to the 1108 taper-lock bushing and timing pulley.

steps.title: Install set screws in the correct holes
steps.purpose: Ensure the set screws are placed in the correct opposing holes for proper taper lock installation.
steps.instruction: Thread set screws into the two holes opposite each other. Use the holes that have threads on the pulley half and are smooth on the bushing half.
steps.expected_result: Both set screws are installed in the correct opposing holes.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected holes are opposite each other and match the threaded-on-pulley, smooth-on-bushing configuration.
steps.failure_condition: The set screws cannot be threaded into the specified holes or the hole selection does not match the documented configuration.

actions.label: Thread set screws
actions.exact_action: Thread set screws into the two holes opposite each other; use the holes that have threads on the pulley half and are smooth on the bushing half.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Set screw locations and pulley versus bushing features used to identify the correct opposing holes.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the correct opposing holes cannot be identified.
Stop if the set screws do not thread into the documented pulley-threaded, bushing-smooth holes.

source_evidence.text: Thread set screws into the two holes opposite each other; use the holes that have threads on the pulley half and are smooth on the bushing half.
Figure 7-20 is associated with timing pulley replacement and includes labeled references to set screws and the 1108 taper-lock bushing.

steps.title: Install pulley assembly onto gearbox shaft
steps.purpose: Place the pulley assembly onto the shaft with the keyway correctly aligned to the gearbox shaft key when present.
steps.instruction: Slip the assembly onto the shaft with the keyway slot aligned with the gearbox shaft key, if present.
steps.expected_result: The pulley assembly is installed onto the shaft with the keyway aligned to the shaft key if present.
steps.healthy_condition: The assembly slides onto the shaft and the keyway alignment is maintained.
steps.failure_condition: The assembly cannot be slipped onto the shaft or the keyway cannot be aligned with the gearbox shaft key.

actions.label: Fit assembly to shaft
actions.exact_action: Slip the assembly onto the shaft with the keyway slot aligned with the gearbox shaft key, if present.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing pulley assembly orientation relative to the gearbox shaft and pilot face.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the pulley cannot be aligned with the gearbox shaft key.

source_evidence.text: Slip the assembly onto the shaft with the keyway slot aligned with the gearbox shaft key, if present.

steps.title: Set pulley spacing from gearbox pilot face
steps.purpose: Position the pulley at the documented final distance from the gearbox pilot face.
steps.instruction: Position the pulley so the final location is 8 mm (0.315 in.) from the gearbox pilot face. A spacer may be placed between the gearbox pilot face and pulley to maintain the spacing, and an 8-mm hex wrench can be used for this task.
steps.expected_result: The pulley is positioned 8 mm (0.315 in.) from the gearbox pilot face.
steps.healthy_condition: The final pulley location is maintained at the documented 8 mm gap.
steps.failure_condition: The 8 mm (0.315 in.) spacing cannot be achieved or maintained.

actions.label: Position pulley gap
actions.exact_action: Position the pulley so the final location is 8 mm (0.315 in.) from the gearbox pilot face.

actions.label: Use spacer or hex wrench as gauge
actions.exact_action: A spacer may be placed between the gearbox pilot face and pulley to maintain the spacing, and an 8-mm hex wrench can be used for this task.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The labeled gearbox pilot face and the 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap between the pilot face and timing pulley.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the documented 8 mm spacing cannot be achieved.

source_evidence.text: Position the pulley so the final location is 8 mm (0.315 in.) from the gearbox pilot face; a spacer may be placed between the gearbox pilot face and pulley to maintain the spacing, and an 8-mm hex wrench can be used for this task.
Figure 7-20 includes labeled references to the gearbox pilot face and an 8 mm (0.315 in.) gap.

steps.title: Tighten set screws alternately to final torque
steps.purpose: Secure the pulley assembly evenly and to the documented final torque.
steps.instruction: Tighten both set screws in small increments, alternating between them until the set screw torque reaches 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb).
steps.expected_result: Both set screws are tightened evenly to 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb).
steps.healthy_condition: The set screws reach the documented torque using the alternating tightening pattern.
steps.failure_condition: The set screws cannot be tightened in the documented alternating pattern to 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb).

actions.label: Alternate set screw tightening
actions.exact_action: Tighten both set screws in small increments, alternating between them.

actions.label: Torque set screws
actions.exact_action: Continue until the set screw torque reaches 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb).

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Set screw locations on the timing pulley assembly while applying alternating tightening.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the set screws cannot be tightened in the documented alternating pattern to 5.6 Nm.
Stop if final torque of 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb) cannot be achieved.

source_evidence.text: Tighten both set screws in small increments, alternating between them until the set screw torque reaches 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb).

steps.title: Reinstall side panels if needed
steps.purpose: Restore removed paneling after the pulley installation work is complete.
steps.instruction: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels, if necessary.
steps.expected_result: Any removed top and/or bottom side panels are reinstalled.
steps.healthy_condition: Panels that were removed for access are back in place.

actions.label: Reinstall panels
actions.exact_action: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels, if necessary.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if removed panels cannot be reinstalled as needed.

source_evidence.text: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels, if necessary.

steps.title: Restart operator station using referenced procedure
steps.purpose: Return the operator station to service using the referenced startup procedure.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station using the referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66.
steps.expected_result: The operator station restart is performed using the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The restart is handed off to or completed under the referenced startup procedure.
steps.failure_condition: The referenced startup procedure is unavailable or the operator station cannot be restarted using it.

actions.label: Restart operator station
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station using the referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" is not available.
Escalate if the operator station cannot be restarted using the referenced procedure.

source_evidence.text: Re-start the operator station using the referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66.
The restart step is source-backed but depends on a separate referenced procedure on page 66.

success_criteria: The timing pulley is installed on the gearbox shaft.
The taper lock bushing and pulley holes are aligned as documented.
The keyway slot is aligned with the gearbox shaft key, if present.
The pulley final location is 8 mm (0.315 in.) from the gearbox pilot face.
Both set screws are tightened in alternating increments to 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb).
Top and/or bottom side panels are reinstalled as needed.
The operator station restart is performed using the referenced procedure.
healthy_conditions: All three holes align during loose assembly.
Set screws are installed in the correct opposing holes.
Pulley assembly fits onto the shaft with keyway alignment maintained.
The 8 mm (0.315 in.) spacing from the gearbox pilot face is achieved.
Both set screws reach 5.6 Nm (49.5 in-lb) using alternating tightening.
Removed panels are reinstalled as needed.
failure_conditions: Pulley cannot be aligned with the gearbox shaft key.
The documented 8 mm spacing cannot be achieved.
Set screws cannot be tightened in the documented alternating pattern to 5.6 Nm.
The source excerpt does not provide full safety isolation or guarded-access prerequisites.
escalation_guidance: Escalate for higher-skill support review before execution because the excerpt does not include full safety isolation or guarded-access prerequisites.
Stop and escalate if the pulley cannot be aligned with the gearbox shaft key.
Stop and escalate if the documented 8 mm spacing cannot be achieved.
Stop and escalate if the set screws cannot be tightened in the documented alternating pattern to 5.6 Nm.
Escalate if the referenced operator station startup procedure is unavailable or restart cannot be completed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall timing pulley installation figure, including set screws, taper-lock bushing, gearbox pilot face, and 8 mm gap.

missing_details: The source packet does not include the OCR text for page 184 section 7.3.19.2 installation.
The source excerpt does not provide complete safety isolation, LOTO, or guarded-access prerequisites for this installation step sequence.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time for this installation procedure in the supplied packet.
The restart step depends on the separate referenced procedure "Starting The Operator Station" on page 66, which is not included in this packet.
